title: What is ELSS? — Tax Saving Mutual Fund Guide
meta_description: ELSS guide 2026 — tax saving mutual fund under Section 80C with 3-year lock-in. Returns 12-15%, how to invest via SIP, top ELSS funds, and comparison with PPF.

hero.one_liner: The smartest Section 80C investment — equity mutual fund that saves tax AND generates 12-15% returns with the shortest lock-in of just 3 years

sections.heading: Overview
content: ELSS (Equity Linked Saving Scheme) is a type of mutual fund that qualifies for Section 80C tax deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh per year. It has the SHORTEST lock-in period among all 80C options — just 3 years — compared to 5 years for tax-saver FD, 15 years for PPF, and till-retirement for NPS/EPF.

Being an equity fund, ELSS invests primarily in stocks and has historically delivered 12-15% annualized returns over 10+ year periods. This makes it the highest-returning 80C instrument. You get dual benefit: tax saving today + wealth creation over time.

You can invest in ELSS via SIP (₹500/month onwards) or lump sum. Each SIP installment has its own 3-year lock-in from the date of investment. So a January 2026 SIP installment becomes redeemable in January 2029, while February 2026's installment unlocks in February 2029, and so on.

Tax on ELSS returns: Long-term capital gains (after 3 years) above ₹1.25 lakh/year are taxed at 12.5%. Gains up to ₹1.25 lakh/year are tax-free. So if you invest ₹1.5 lakh/year for 3 years and make ₹1 lakh profit, the entire profit is tax-free (below ₹1.25 lakh threshold).

rows.label: 80C deduction
rows.value: Up to ₹1,50,000/year investment qualifies. Tax saved: up to ₹46,800 (30% bracket).

rows.label: Lock-in
rows.value: 3 years from date of each investment (SIP or lump sum)

rows.label: LTCG tax
rows.value: Gains above ₹1.25 lakh/year taxed at 12.5%. Below ₹1.25 lakh: tax-free.

rows.label: Min investment
rows.value: ₹500/month SIP or ₹500 lump sum (varies by fund)

rows.label: Risk
rows.value: Moderate-High (equity risk). Suitable for 3+ year investment horizon.

sections.heading: ELSS vs PPF vs Tax Saver FD vs NPS — 80C Comparison
How to Choose and Invest in ELSS
content: Step 1: Open an account on any mutual fund app (Groww, Zerodha Coin, Kuvera). Complete KYC with PAN + Aadhaar — one-time 10-minute process.

Step 2: Search for ELSS funds. Popular options: Mirae Asset Tax Saver Fund, Axis Long Term Equity, Canara Robeco Equity Tax Saver, Quant Tax Plan, Parag Parikh Tax Saver. Choose 'Direct Growth' plan for lowest expense ratio.
Step 3: Start a monthly SIP of ₹12,500 (₹1,50,000 ÷ 12 months) for full 80C utilization. Or invest a lump sum before March 31 for that year's 80C benefit.

Step 4: After 3 years, each SIP installment automatically unlocks. You can redeem or continue holding. Recommendation: continue holding beyond 3 years for maximum compounding benefit. ELSS doesn't force redemption at 3 years — the lock-in just means you CAN'T sell before 3 years.

Pro tip: Invest ₹12,500/month SIP in ELSS throughout the year instead of lump sum in March. SIP gives rupee cost averaging (better average purchase price over time) and avoids the March rush of investing a large sum at potentially high market prices.

steps.title: Complete KYC on any mutual fund platform
steps.description: Groww, Zerodha Coin, Kuvera — PAN + Aadhaar + bank account. One-time process.

steps.title: Search 'ELSS' or 'Tax Saver' funds
steps.description: Select a fund with good 5-year track record. Choose 'Direct - Growth' option.

steps.title: Claim 80C when filing ITR
steps.description: Download ELSS investment statement as proof. Submit to employer for TDS adjustment.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw ELSS before 3 years?
faqs.answer: No. ELSS has a mandatory 3-year lock-in. You cannot redeem, switch, or transfer ELSS units before 3 years from the date of each investment. This is the trade-off for 80C benefit. After 3 years, you can redeem anytime with no exit load.

faqs.question: I invested ₹1.5 lakh in ELSS and markets crashed. Did I lose the tax benefit?
faqs.answer: No. The 80C deduction is based on the AMOUNT INVESTED, not the current market value. Your ₹1.5 lakh deduction and tax saving are locked in regardless of market performance. Market value affects your returns when you eventually redeem, not your tax benefit.

faqs.question: ELSS SIP — does each installment have a separate 3-year lock-in?
faqs.answer: Yes. This is important to understand. If you invest ₹12,500 in January 2026, that specific installment unlocks in January 2029. February 2026's installment unlocks in February 2029, and so on. After you've been investing for 3+ years, some portion of your ELSS becomes redeemable every month.

faqs.question: What if I need the money within 3 years?
faqs.answer: You cannot access ELSS money before 3 years. If you might need the money sooner, don't invest it in ELSS. Use a 5-year tax saver FD (slightly less returns but guaranteed) or PPF (allows loan from 3rd year). Only invest in ELSS the amount you can lock in for 3+ years.

faqs.question: Should I invest in one ELSS fund or multiple?
faqs.answer: One or maximum two ELSS funds is enough. Multiple equity funds with similar mandates just create overlap without adding diversification. A single well-chosen ELSS fund already holds 40-80 stocks across sectors. Adding a second one means you're holding 80-160 stocks with significant overlap.

disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only. Consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
